title: NOC 43203 – Border Services, Customs, and Immigration Officers | GTR Immigration
description: Border Services, Customs, and Immigration Officers under NOC 43203 play a critical role in protecting Canada’s borders, enforcing customs regulations, and administering immigration laws. Classified under TEER 3, this occupation ensures national security, public safety, and lawful entry of people and goods into Canada. These professionals are primarily employed by federal government agencies such as the Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA).

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER3 – Occupations usually require a college diploma of less than two years or more than six months of on-the-job trainingMajor Group43 – Assisting occupations in education and in legal and public protectionSub-Major Group432 – Assisting occupations in legal and public protectionMinor Group4320 – Assisting occupations in legal and public protectionNOC Code43203

duties: Conduct interviews and inspections at border entry points
Apply immigration, customs, plant, animal, and food inspection legislation
Inspect baggage and cargo for contraband or undeclared goods
Board carriers arriving from foreign countries
Operate firearms and apply defensive tactics when necessary
Conduct arrests, detentions, or searches under applicable laws
Determine admissibility of persons entering Canada
Grant permanent resident status or order detention/deportation
Locate and apprehend individuals violating immigration laws
Appear as witnesses in immigration appeal cases
employment_requirements: Completion of specialized federal government training
Approximately 4.5 months of specialized training at the CBSA College

Successful completion of:Officer Trainee Entrance Exam (OTEE)Canadian Firearms Safety Course (CFSC)Canadian Restricted Firearms Safety Course (CRFSC)Psychological assessmentPhysical Abilities Requirement Evaluation (PARE)Medical examinationControl and Defensive Tactics (CDT) training
Security clearance and background checks are mandatory.
salary_prospects: Border Services, Customs, and Immigration Officers typically earn:
$48,200 – $87,800 per year,depending on experience, province, and seniority.
labour_market_demand: Border Services, Customs, and Immigration Officers are employed by:
Airports, land borders, and marine ports
Growth in international trade and travel
Opportunities for advancement into investigative or supervisory roles
Border and immigration enforcement remain essential public service sectors in Canada.
pathways: Border Services, Customs, and Immigration Officers fall under TEER 3 skilled occupations, potentially qualifying for:
Express Entry (if eligibility criteria are met)
Successful completion of required government training
If you are planning to build a career in border, customs, or immigration enforcement in Canada, contact GTR Immigration today to explore your pathway to Canada PR under NOC 43203.
